name: Do I really need multiple bids for small handyman jobs?
headline: Do I really need multiple bids for small handyman jobs?

mainEntity.name: Do I really need multiple bids for small handyman jobs?
mainEntity.text: Do I really need multiple bids for small handyman jobs?

acceptedAnswer.text: For most smaller handyman jobs, you do not need multiple bids. But for **larger or more expensive projects**, getting at least **two or three estimates** is smart. Consider getting multiple bids when: - The job will cost more than you’re comfortable paying without comparison (for many people, this might be over a few hundred dollars) - The work is unclear and could be done in different ways - You’ve never hired anyone for this type of project before Multiple bids help you: - See if one price is much higher or lower than others - Compare how each person plans to do the work - Judge professionalism from their communication and level of detail Pitfalls: - Choosing only the cheapest bid. A very low price can mean shortcuts, cheap materials, or “surprise” add‑on charges later. - Asking for many detailed quotes when you only plan to hire the absolute cheapest person. That can waste people’s time and may lead to rushed or careless estimates. For small, clear tasks (like hanging shelves, fixing a door, or installing a faucet), you may be fine calling one well‑reviewed handyman. For anything that changes your home in a big way or involves risk (water, electricity, structure), comparing at least a couple of bids is usually worth it.

description: For most smaller handyman jobs, you do not need multiple bids. But for **larger or more expensive projects**, getting at least **two or three estimates** is sma
